source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Shoulder Injury During Coil Tubing Operations

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Garden Banks Block 506

actions_taken: IP evacuated via medevac helicopter at 1010 hours
BSEE conducted onsite investigation on 20 March 2024
IP diagnosed with dislocated shoulder and torn labrum
IP released to restricted work duty
G-110 Incident of Non-Compliance issued to Talos ERT LLC on 2 April 2024
event.summary: On 20 March 2024 at approximately 0705 hours, a Schlumberger Pump Operator sustained a left shoulder injury on the Helix Q-4000 semisubmersible vessel during well workover operations. The injured party attempted to jump across a four-foot gap with a one-foot eight-inch rise in elevation from the Coil Tubing Control Cabin to the Coil Tubing Reel Platform to install a Universal Tubing Length Monitor. During the transition, the IP lost his footing and started to fall. He grabbed the Coil Tubing Cabin crash frame with his left hand, injuring his left shoulder. The IP was wearing fall protection harness with self-retracting lanyard. Investigation determined the IP failed to follow the Job Safety Analysis which required use of a ladder for access.
recommendations: Talos ERT LLC must provide a letter of explanation within 14 days to the Lafayette District Well Operations Supervisor along with plans to prevent future incidents
key_phrases: failed to follow working at heights Job Safety Analysis
attempted to jump across a 4-foot gap
grabbed the Coil Tubing Cabin crash frame
failed to operate in a safe and workmanlike manner

hazards.name: Working at Heights - Elevated Platform Access

threats.name: Deviation from Job Safety Analysis - Ladder Not Used
Attempting to Jump Across Gap Between Platforms
Loss of Footing During Platform Transition

consequences.name: Personnel Injury - Shoulder Dislocation and Torn Labrum

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis - Working at Heights Requirements
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control requiring use of ladder for platform access
controls.lod_basis: Primary procedural control to prevent unsafe access methods

supporting_text: The BSEE Incident Investigation Team concluded that the IP failed to follow the Schlumberger working at heights Job Safety Analysis
The Job Safety Analysis called for the use of a ladder to access the Coil Tubing Guide Counter

Schlumberger Pump Operator, the IP, failed to follow working at heights requirements as stated in the Working at Heights section of the Job Safety Analysis that included making use of a ladder of adequate height

controls.name: Permit to Work System
controls.barrier_role: Authorization and verification of safe work procedures
controls.lod_basis: Primary administrative control for work authorization

supporting_text: BSEE met with Talos representatives and gathered incident related documents including Schlumberger Job Safety Analysis, Schlumberger Permit to Work

controls.name: Fall Protection Harness with Self-Retracting Lanyard
controls.barrier_role: Personal protective equipment to arrest fall
controls.lod_basis: Secondary protection to mitigate consequences of fall

supporting_text: BSEE learned that at the time of the incident, the IP was wearing a fall protection harness attached with a self-retracting lanyard for vertical fall protection
the Schlumberger Pump Operator, the Injured Person (IP), had stepped outside of the Coil Tubing Cabin crash frame while attached to a fall protection harness with a Self-Retracting Lanyard

controls.name: Ladder for Platform Access
controls.barrier_role: Physical means to safely access elevated platform
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineered means of safe access

supporting_text: The Job Safety Analysis called for the use of a ladder to access the Coil Tubing Guide Counter
The IP made the decision to jump from the Coil Tubing Control Cabin to the Coil Tubing Reel Platform

Schlumberger Pump Operator injured his left shoulder while attempting to cross over from the Coil Tubing Control Cabin onto the Coil Tubing Reel Platform without the use of a required ladder as stated in the Job Safety Analysis

controls.name: Safe and Workmanlike Manner Operations Requirement
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory baseline requirement for all operations

supporting_text: A G-110 Incident of Non-Compliance was issued on 2 April 2024 to document that Talos ERT LLC Corporation (Talos) failed to comply with the regulatory requirement to operate in a safe and workmanlike manner

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
